purpose: Track deferred/incomplete acceptance criteria and known-by-inspection gaps from shipped phases. Reference this file when a converter / agent / pipeline regression surfaces — the answer may already be cataloged here. Add an entry whenever a phase ships with a soft acceptance criterion or a speculative gap that real-fixture data would close.

items.title: LEGO real-fixture validation of is_physical_card_surface
items.summary: 50.7 plan acceptance criterion #4 ('LEGO membership card section #16 detects 3+ signals -> is_physical=True') was not mechanically verified against real LEGO data. Detector is validated end-to-end only against synthetic DesignNode fixtures (test_lego_membership_card_pattern uses a hand-shaped tree).
code_refs: app/design_sync/figma/layout_analyzer.py:397-414 (wiring; only fires when inner_bg is not None)
items.symptom_if_broken: Phase 52.7's Rule 9 dark-mode flip will incorrectly invert the LEGO Insiders membership card (and other physical-card surfaces) in dark mode, because is_physical_card_surface returns False on real Figma extractions even though the card visually qualifies.
items.closes_when: Either (a) Phase 53.6 promotes LEGO into data/debug/lego/ as a regression case with manifest expectation 'sections[16].is_physical_card_surface: true', OR (b) Phase 51A pulls a slim 51.0 LEGO-promotion task forward as a prereq for 51.2/51.3/51.6.
items.fast_closure_path: Parse the cached raw_figma JSON via app.design_sync.figma.adapter.figma_to_design_node(), pass through analyze_layout(), assert sections[16].is_physical_card_surface is True. ~30-line one-off script — no live Figma PAT required.

items.title: _has_logo_on_white_field walks at most 2 nesting levels
items.summary: Detector finds a logo image only when nested as 'card -> white-frame -> image' or 'card -> white-frame' (image is the white frame itself). Real Figma exports often add a padding wrapper between the card and the white field, producing 'card -> padding-frame -> white-frame -> image'. Detector misses the logo signal in that shape.
items.symptom_if_broken: Physical cards with a wrapped-white logo field score one fewer signal than expected. With only 1 of 4 signals firing, is_physical=False, and Rule 9 incorrectly dark-flips them. Particularly likely on cards with explicit padding tokens applied at multiple nesting depths.
items.closes_when: Either (a) AC #4 closure surfaces this empirically and the fix lands as a follow-up, OR (b) someone writes a recursive walker mirroring _walk_image_descendants but bounded by depth (e.g. 4 levels) and adds a regression test with a real fixture.
items.fix_sketch: Replace the `for child in card.children` loop with a depth-bounded BFS that examines every FRAME with a white fill at any depth <=4, then checks its descendants for image children with area <= 60% of the white field's area.

items.title: Detection gated on inner_bg being set by 50.4
items.summary: Wiring at layout_analyzer.py:403 only runs detect_physical_card_surface when 50.4's _detect_inner_bg returns a non-None inner_bg. If the LEGO membership card is structured as a top-level section without an outer coloured wrapper (e.g. the card IS the entire footer area, not nested inside a coloured band), inner_bg=None and detection never fires — regardless of how many signals would have matched.
items.symptom_if_broken: Same as Gap 2: is_physical_card_surface=False on real cards even though the detector's signal logic would have classified them correctly if invoked.
items.closes_when: AC #4 closure determines whether LEGO's section #16 has inner_bg populated. If not, either (a) relax the gate to fire on any section with corner_radius >= 16 + barcode/QR descendant, OR (b) extend 50.4's _detect_inner_bg to populate inner_bg for top-level cards (section IS the card).

items.title: pyright reportDeprecated demoted to warning to absorb @asynccontextmanager false-positive in 1.1.409
items.summary: pyright 1.1.409's bundled typeshed marks contextlib.asynccontextmanager / contextmanager as deprecated. Under strict mode (typeCheckingMode='strict') this fires reportDeprecated=error on every decorated generator and breaks `make types`. Workaround: pyproject.toml sets `reportDeprecated = 'warning'` instead of patching every call site or pinning pyright back. The narrower fix is to annotate each decorated function as `-> AsyncGenerator[T]` / `-> Generator[T]` (the typeshed deprecation only fires when the return type is a bare generator), which sidesteps the deprecation entirely without weakening the project-wide rule.
items.symptom_if_broken: A genuinely deprecated API (e.g. a Pydantic v3 / SQLAlchemy 2.x removal) lands in a future bump and pyright now logs it as a warning instead of failing `make types` — the regression slips through CI. Less severe but possible: another false-positive deprecation upstream goes unnoticed because the project-wide demote masks it.
items.closes_when: The 5 cited @asynccontextmanager / @contextmanager call sites annotate their return types as `AsyncGenerator[Foo]` / `Generator[Foo]` (or upstream typeshed reverts the deprecation marker), AND `reportDeprecated` returns to its strict-mode default (`error`).
items.fix_sketch: For each cited site, change `async def lifespan(app):` -> `async def lifespan(app) -> AsyncGenerator[None]:` and `def fixture():` -> `def fixture() -> Generator[Foo]:`. Then drop `reportDeprecated = 'warning'` from pyproject.toml:324 and re-run `make types` to confirm green under the strict default.

items.title: 3 of 7 for_converter_engine templates have no structure.json — never validated
items.summary: email-templates/training_HTML/for_converter_engine/ holds 7 reference templates: Lego, maap, mammut, performance_reimagined, reframe_2025, slate, Starbucks. Phase 50 wired 4 of them into data/debug/ as regression cases (maap=5, Starbucks=6, mammut=10, reframe_2025=reframe). The other 3 — Lego, performance_reimagined, slate — have figma_link.txt + visual_design.png but no extracted structure.json, so they validate nothing. Phase 50 cited LEGO heavily as the visual oracle (master plan §6, §201; 50.4 plan §208/219; 50.7 AC #4) but the data needed to enforce that oracle was never captured.
items.symptom_if_broken: Any converter regression that affects LEGO / slate / performance_reimagined patterns ships without being caught. Examples: Phase 51A composite-slot work (51.2/51.3/51.6) targeting LEGO's footer membership card, or any future Rule (52.x) whose canonical example lives in one of the 3 stranded templates.
items.closes_when: Phase 53.6 promotes all 3 into data/debug/ — OR a slim 51.0 task pulls LEGO forward as a Phase 51A prereq, with performance_reimagined and slate following in 53.6.

available_data.performance_reimagined: no cached extraction — needs live Figma access
available_data.slate: no cached extraction — needs live Figma access

items.fast_closure_path: For LEGO: ~30-line script using the cached JSON (see phase-50.7-ac-4.fast_closure_path). For perf_reimagined + slate: live Figma extract via `python -m app.design_sync.diagnose.extract --connection-id <N> --node-id <NODE>` then save to data/debug/{slug}/structure.json + tokens.json + manifest.yaml.

tooling_followups.title: Wire deferred-items.json scan into /preflight-check
tooling_followups.trigger: When open_items count reaches >= 5 (currently 5 — threshold met, ready to wire)
tooling_followups.summary: Add a step to .claude/commands/preflight-check.md that grep s .agents/deferred-items.json for entries whose phase or code_refs overlap the plan's 'Files to Create/Modify' list, and surfaces matches in the preflight report. This converts the convention from 'rule the model must remember' to 'mechanical check that runs before /be-execute'.
tooling_followups.acceptance: Preflight report includes a 'Deferred Items Touching This Plan' section listing matching entries with id, summary, and severity. Empty section when no matches — never silent.

phase_50_summary.regression_baseline: make converter-data-regression -> 38 passed, 24 skipped, 0 failed (4 cases: MAAP, Starbucks, Mammut, REFRAME). LEGO + performance_reimagined + slate not in regression suite — see phase-50-stranded-templates.
